Package simulation

Class Environment

java.lang.Object
simulation.Environment
public final class Environment extends Object
The simulation grid and sensor-node registry.

Owns the raw int[][] field grid (where a cell holds the node ID that occupies it, or 0 if empty) and a Map<Integer, NodeAgent> agent registry indexed by node ID.

Range queries — no O(width × height) scan

findAgentsInRange(int, int, int) iterates over the agent registry in O(n) time (n = deployed node count), not over the full grid. This replaces the O(width × height) field scans used for neighbour detection in the legacy Run.individual_distance(), Run.drone_distance(), and Run.waypoint_distance() methods.

Single source of truth for position

The grid cell and the NodeAgent's own (x, y) fields are always kept in sync: NodeMover updates both atomically. Any caller that needs a node's position should read from the agent directly (agent.getX() / agent.getY()) rather than scanning the field.

  • Constructor Details

    • Environment

      public Environment(int fieldWidth, int fieldHeight)
      Constructs an empty simulation environment.
      Parameters:
      fieldWidth - number of rows in the grid (≥ 1)
      fieldHeight - number of columns in the grid (≥ 1)

  • Method Details

    • deployAgent

      public boolean deployAgent(NodeAgent agent)
      Deploys a sensor-node agent into the environment.

      Writes the agent's ID into field[agent.getX()][agent.getY()] and registers it in the agent map.

      Parameters:
      agent - the node to deploy
      Returns:
      true if deployment succeeded; false if the target cell was already occupied

    • setCell

      public boolean setCell(int x, int y, int value)
      Writes an arbitrary value into a cell.

      Used to place the UAV sentinel (UAV.UAV_ID = 747) at its starting position before the simulation begins.

      Parameters:
      x - row index
      y - column index
      value - value to write
      Returns:
      true if the cell was empty and the write succeeded

    • getField

      public int[][] getField()
      Returns the raw simulation grid.

      The reference is intentionally direct (not defensive-copied) so that NodeMover and UAV can update cells in-place.

      Returns:
      the grid array (field[row][col])

    • getFieldWidth

      public int getFieldWidth()
      Returns:
      number of rows in the grid

    • getFieldHeight

      public int getFieldHeight()
      Returns:
      number of columns in the grid

    • allAgents

      public Collection<NodeAgent> allAgents()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of all deployed sensor-node agents, in deployment order.
      Returns:
      ordered, unmodifiable collection of agents

    • agentById

      public Map<Integer,NodeAgent> agentById()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the agent registry.

      Used by KnowledgeBase.observeWithTransitiveCache(java.util.List<simulation.NodeAgent>, java.util.Map<java.lang.Integer, simulation.NodeAgent>) for O(1) neighbour lookups by ID.

      Returns:
      unmodifiable map from node ID to NodeAgent

    • findAgentsInRange

      public List<NodeAgent> findAgentsInRange(int cx, int cy, int range)
      Returns all sensor-node agents within Euclidean distance range of (cx, cy), excluding any agent located exactly at that point (squared distance = 0, i.e. a node sitting at the same cell).

      Distance is compared using squared integer arithmetic to avoid floating-point overhead. The inclusive upper bound (≤ range²) matches the root_mid[i][j] <= range_of_nodes check from the legacy Run.individual_distance().

      Runs in O(n) time over the agent registry — no O(width × height) field scan.

      Parameters:
      cx - centre row
      cy - centre column
      range - detection radius (grid cells, inclusive)
      Returns:
      mutable list of agents within range; may be empty
